title: anthemis cretica subsp. sibthorpii
intro: Anthemis cretica subsp. sibthorpii, also known as Sibthorp's chamomile, is a subspecies of Anthemis cretica, a flowering plant native to the Balkan Peninsula. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and calming effects, making it useful in soothing digestive issues and reducing anxiety. The health benefits of Sibthorp's chamomile include its ability to aid in sleep, reduce stress and inflammation, and provide relief from menstrual cramps and skin irritations. In herbal preparations, Sibthorp's chamomile is often used in teas, infusions, and topical creams to harness its therapeutic properties.
